$comment: Source of truth for the apodictic:scene_roster block — the PRODUCER for Viz Chart 5 (character co-presence network; docs/manuscript-visualizations.md §The chart set, chart 5). ONE block per manuscript. It records, per Timeline scene, which characters are PRESENT and acting on-page — NOT who is merely mentioned. PRESENT-NOT-MENTIONED RULE: a character is present in a scene iff they take on-page action, speak, or are a POV/thought subject WITHIN the scene's Timeline line-range; a character named only in another's speech/thought, in narrative summary, or by reference is MENTIONED -> no roster entry, no co-presence edge. This reading is PRODUCER/AUTHOR-ENFORCED (a validator cannot read prose to adjudicate presence — the Continuity Bible C2 author-enforced precedent) and made ACCOUNTABLE by the REQUIRED, non-empty `anchor` (a Timeline-relative line-range or a short on-page quote where the character acts). VERBATIM-COPY DISCIPLINE: the manifest's co_presence[].characters are bare canonical names copied from these rosters and byte-checked against them (viz_manifest.py X2); the renderer computes edges/weights MECHANICALLY (an edge iff two names co-occur in >=1 roster; weight = co-occurrence count) and invents nothing. PROVENANCE-CLOSED: every roster scene_id must resolve to a Timeline Event-Ledger row, and the scene's Timeline POV character must appear in its roster (a roster missing its own POV is an error). NAME NORMALIZATION: no machine-readable character-identity artifact exists (character-architecture.md is prose), so this block ships an inline `character_aliases` table (declared surface-form -> canonical-name); the co-occurrence + render key on canonical names. additionalProperties:false — a stray top-level key is a typo. The nested rosters[]/characters[]/character_aliases[] objects are validated by viz_manifest.py (the subset engine does not recurse). The GATE enforces anchor-non-empty + provenance closure, NOT the semantic present-vs-mentioned reading. Validated by scripts/viz_manifest.py (X2). See docs/manuscript-visualizations.md.
title: APODICTIC scene roster (apodictic.scene_roster.v1)
